why_them: The site runs on WordPress with Golf Channel Solutions but has a broken gallery slider displaying an error where the course imagery should appear — a direct credibility hit for a club selling a visual experience. Accommodation is referenced in the navigation but no details are rendered. Overall the site functions but carries visible technical faults that undermine the premium positioning the club is trying to project.
recommended_service: Website redesign or audit with gallery fix, photography integration, and a modern WordPress rebuild
outreach_hook: Your gallery slider is broken on the homepage — visitors see an error instead of your course.

location: Golf Course Road, Portpatrick, Wigtownshire, DG9 8TB
why_them: Built on the generic Club Systems International template with a broken Members App link and a Junior Coaching link with no destination — both dead ends for users trying to engage. Green fee availability is not surfaced on the homepage for visitors despite society bookings being heavily promoted. The platform is template-bound, limiting SEO potential and giving no differentiation against other D&G golf clubs.
recommended_service: Custom website redesign with a proper visitor booking flow, SEO-optimised landing pages, and working junior coaching section
outreach_hook: Your Members App and Junior Coaching links are broken — two dead ends pushing visitors away.

why_them: Built on WordPress with Divi but renders almost no visible content — the homepage delivers CSS and JavaScript framework code rather than usable text, meaning search engines and visitors see a near-blank page. No online booking system or membership sign-up exists. The 1893-founded club has genuine appeal and strong reviews but its digital presence is severely undercutting visitor conversion.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with proper SEO content, online tee booking integration, and an online membership enquiry form
outreach_hook: Your website is nearly invisible to search engines — visitors see code instead of your course.

why_them: Built on WordPress with Divi but renders only framework code — no contact details, pricing, or booking system are surfaced to visitors from the homepage. BRS Golf handles tee booking via an external URL rather than an integrated on-site system. The club's setting beside Kirk Loch with strong wildlife interest has clear marketing potential that the current site entirely fails to exploit.
recommended_service: Website redesign with SEO-friendly content, integrated booking, membership system, and nature tourism angle
outreach_hook: Your homepage shows no content to Google — the club's scenic setting is completely invisible online.

why_them: Built on the DotGolf platform with tee booking and an online membership form, but the Latest News section prominently displays 'No results found' — a visible failure state on the homepage signalling a neglected CMS. The DotGolf template is generic with no distinctive visual identity to separate Moffat from other Scottish hill-course clubs.
recommended_service: SEO audit and content strategy, news feed fix, and brand-differentiated landing pages targeting Moffat's visitor tourism market
outreach_hook: Your homepage news section shows 'No results found' — it's the first thing visitors notice.

location: Gatehouse of Fleet, Castle Douglas, DG7 2DJ
why_them: Runs on WordPress with the default Twenty Twenty-One theme — a placeholder site rather than a purposeful club presence. No contact details appear on the homepage and no online membership portal or sign-up process is visible despite the club operating multiple teams. Fixture data and a live calendar are not surfaced to visitors.
recommended_service: Sports club website build with fixtures integration, membership sign-up form, and local SEO
outreach_hook: Your website uses a default WordPress theme and shows no contact details — new players can't find you.

location: Park Farm, New Abbey Road, Dumfries, DG2 7LU
why_them: Built on Craft CMS with a spelling error on a published event and no match results or league standings visible. For a 500-player club with men's, women's, and youth teams, the site punches well below the club's actual scale and ambition. No online merchandise or ticketing is available.
recommended_service: Website overhaul with fixtures feed, youth recruitment landing pages, and online membership sign-up
outreach_hook: Your site has a published spelling error and no match results — it doesn't reflect a 500-player club.

location: London Road Playing Fields, Stranraer, DG9 8BN
why_them: Runs on Pitchero's off-the-shelf template with no visual differentiation from hundreds of other rugby club sites. No email address is publicly listed anywhere on the site, making it impossible for potential sponsors, new players, or touring teams to contact the club without phoning. The membership system is not accessible on public pages.
recommended_service: Custom website replacing Pitchero template with visible contact email, sponsor acquisition page, and junior rugby recruitment section
outreach_hook: There's no email address anywhere on your website — sponsors and new players have no way to reach you.
